notes: Compact thermal penetrator systems for direct in-situ water extraction at ice-rich sites (8%+ water ice).
A resistive or microwave-driven drill head sublimates subsurface ice deposits in place.
Evolved vapor rises through a sealed capture hood into a cryocondenser module for collection.
Output is a flat rated water production regardless of ice concentration — ice fraction gates deployment only.
Lightweight units (2.5–68 t) but very high power draw — converting electricity to thermal energy at scale.
Multiple units at the same facility sum their extraction rates.
Suited for permanently shadowed craters, Ceres, cometary bodies, and other ice-rich locations.
